description: QuantAI - Real SSE Streaming: Replace the current /api/assistant/chat route (which just JSON-proxies) with a proper SSE streaming proxy that forwards the backend stream to the frontend. The useAIChat hook already has full SSE processing logic (processSSEStream) - it just needs the API route to actually stream. Also wire the model selector to pass the selected model through to the backend, and ensure tool-call cards render from the stream.
steps: Step 1: Rewrite apps/quantai/src/app/api/assistant/chat/route.ts to be a streaming proxy. When the request body has stream:true, fetch from the backend with the same body but return a streaming Response using ReadableStream that pipes the backend SSE data through (TransformStream). Set headers Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive. When stream is not requested, fall back to JSON proxy as before.

Step 2: Add a new route apps/quantai/src/app/api/ai/stream/route.ts as an alternative SSE endpoint that always streams. Use the same streaming proxy logic. This provides a dedicated streaming URL separate from the REST chat endpoint.

Step 3: In apps/quantai/src/hooks/useAIChat.ts, verify the fetch call to /api/assistant/chat sends stream:true in the body (it already does). The processSSEStream already handles text/event-stream responses. The key fix is the route returning SSE - the hook code is already correct.

Step 4: Wire the ModelSelector component to actually call the /api/models route to fetch available models from the backend (currently the useModelSelector hook uses a hardcoded AVAILABLE_MODELS list). Add a useModels() React Query hook that fetches /api/models and falls back to the static list on error. Update the ModelSelector component to use this data.

Step 5: Ensure the ToolCallCard component in apps/quantai/src/components/ToolCallCard.tsx renders properly for tool_call events from the stream. The useAIChat hook already calls updateLastAssistantToolCalls. Verify AgenticMessage.tsx renders ToolCallCard for each tool call in the message.
Step 6: Add an .env.local.example file in apps/quantai/ with QUANTAI_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:3020.
Step 7: Add a timeout and abort controller for the streaming proxy so long-running streams can be cancelled (match the stopStreaming() behavior in the hook with AbortController).
acceptance_criteria: apps/quantai/src/app/api/assistant/chat/route.ts returns text/event-stream Content-Type when stream:true is in the request body
The streaming response pipes backend SSE data through to the client in real-time
The useAIChat hook's existing processSSEStream logic works with the new streaming route without modification
Model selector fetches from /api/models and displays available models
Tool call events from the stream render as ToolCallCard components
TypeScript compiles without errors: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/quantai
